title: panicum sanguinale subsp. marginatum
intro: Panicum sanguinale subsp. marginatum, commonly known as red-top panic grass, is a subspecies of grass in the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include treating fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as being used to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. Panicum sanguinale subsp. marginatum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal use, and its leaves and stems can be dried and stored for later use.
